description:
Goal
Add the runtime and heap primitive that makes the correct compiler model possible: one operation that tries to reuse an existing cons cell for `[new_head | new_tail]`, falling back to allocation when reuse is unsafe.

Why this must be first
Current runtime support is tail-only: `fz_list_reuse_or_cons_tail_ref(process, list_ref, tail_ref)` preserves the old head and only relinks the tail. That is insufficient for map-like reconstruction and forces compiler/codegen special cases. The right abstraction is a single generalized operation that can rewrite both head and tail when the source cons is unaliased.

Data/API changes
- In `runtime/src/any_value.rs`, add a `ListCons` mutation method that can update head raw bits, head `ValueKind`, and tail bits together while preserving the alias/publication metadata correctly.
- In `runtime/src/heap/imp.rs`, add something equivalent to `reuse_or_alloc_list_cons(source_cons, new_head_raw, new_head_kind, new_tail)`.
- The heap method must:
  - reject non-list source refs;
  - convert/validate the tail with the same list-tail rules used by allocation;
  - if the source cons is unaliased, rewrite the existing cell head+tail and return the same list ref;
  - if aliased/published, allocate a fresh cons with the supplied head+tail and leave the original cons unchanged.
- In `runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs`, expose one extern symbol for native codegen. Prefer one primitive taking raw head bits plus a compact kind value over a family of per-kind symbols, because `ListCons::new(head_raw, head_kind, tail_bits)` already has that shape.
- In compiler2 native runtime bindings, add the symbol id and a helper call method, but do not yet route list construction through it unless this ticket also updates all callers cleanly.

Deletion target
Once downstream codegen is converted, delete `fz_list_reuse_or_cons_tail_ref` and heap/codegen helpers that only support tail relink. If this ticket cannot delete them because callers still exist, the follow-up codegen ticket must delete them; do not leave them as an adapter layer.

Tests
- Add/extend runtime heap tests:
  - unaliased source cons: returned ref is the same physical cons and both head and tail change;
  - aliased/published source cons: returned ref is fresh, original head/tail remain unchanged;
  - changed head kind works for at least a scalar/value-ref contrast if the runtime representation supports it.
- Run targeted runtime tests for heap/list cons reuse.
- Run `cargo test --workspace` before close unless an existing open ticket documents an unrelated timeout/ignore.

Acceptance
A standalone runtime primitive exists that can safely implement FP2-style cons reuse for both same-head and changed-head reconstruction. It is not wired by a shim; the API shape is the final shape native codegen should call.
title: Runtime: generalize cons reuse to rewrite head and tail with alias fallback
